nodes.x: I cried unto God with my voice, even unto God with my voice; and he gave ear unto me.
In my distress I cried unto the Lord, and he heard me.
In my distress I called upon the Lord, and cried unto my God: he heard my voice out of his temple, and my cry came before him, even into his ears.
I cried unto the Lord with my voice; with my voice unto the Lord did I make my supplication.
In my distress I called upon the Lord, and cried to my God: and he did hear my voice out of his temple, and my cry did enter into his ears.
And said, I cried by reason of mine affliction unto the Lord, and he heard me; out of the belly of hell cried I, and thou heardest my voice.
In the day of my trouble I sought the Lord: my sore ran in the night, and ceased not: my soul refused to be comforted.
Wherein I suffer trouble, as an evil doer, even unto bonds; but the word of God is not bound.

And it came to pass that I was three days and three nights in the most bitter pain and anguish of soul; and never, until I did cry out unto the Lord Jesus Christ for mercy, did I receive a remission of my sins. But behold, I did cry unto him and I did find peace to my soul.
Thou hast thrust sore at me that I might fall: but the Lord helped me.
Unless the Lord had been my help, my soul had almost dwelt in silence.

man,  like  a  slave,  submits  himself  to  another,  and  surrenders 
liimself  to  the  endurance  of  pains  as  to  intolerable  mistresses, 
being  unable  to  look  them  in  the  face,  and  wholly  incapable  of 
conceiving  any  masculine  or  free  thoughts.  On  which  account 
a  countless  number  of  painful  things  are  endured  by  him 
through  the  medium  of  the  outward …
I remembered God, and was troubled: I complained, and my spirit was overwhelmed. Selah.
I cried unto the Lord with my voice, and he heard me out of his holy hill. Selah.
Hath God forgotten to be gracious? hath he in anger shut up his tender mercies? Selah.
Thy horrid downfall shall the righteous s$e, 1 And laugh to scorn thy cursing and thy groans; “Behold the brute who did our Godjdefy! ;; Despised the widow's tears, and orphan’s moans. — ’’Selah!
He shall send from heaven, and save me from the reproach of him that would swallow me up. Selah. God shall send forth his mercy and his truth.
God be merciful unto us, and bless us; and cause his face to shine upon us; Selah.
Thou holdest mine eyes waking: I am so troubled that I cannot speak.
Mine eyes fail for thy word, saying, When wilt thou comfort me?
Have mercy upon me, O Lord, for I am in trouble: mine eye is consumed with grief, yea, my soul and my belly.
Mine eye mourneth by reason of affliction: Lord, I have called daily upon thee, I have stretched out my hands unto thee.
But behold, verily, verily, I say unto you that mine eyes are upon you. I am in your midst and ye cannot see me;
Then thou scarest me with dreams, and terrifiest me through visions:
I have considered the days of old, the years of ancient times.
Ye observe days, and months, and times, and years.
Time. — 8, F or MV Indistinct notions of the lapse of time, of ages, dates of events, &c.
Are thy days as the days of man? are thy years as man’s days,

Regarding the point of the 364 days to the year which the writer of this
book makes, Charles says that “he did this only through sheer incapacity
for appreciating anything better; for he must have been acquainted with
the solar year of 365¼ days. His acquaintance with the Greek cycles
shows this.... The author’s reckoning of the year at 364 days may be
partly due to his opposition to heathen…
I call to remembrance my song in the night: I commune with mine own heart: and my spirit made diligent search.

An Invitation to Grow By Dwan J. Young Primary General President Annette, your voice has filled this hall with beautiful music as you pled, “Teach me, teach me.” In many parts of the world, I have looked at other children one by one who were singing this same song. (See “Teach Me to Walk in the Light,” Sing with Me, p. B-45.) I prayed then, as I pray now, that there is someone close-by who is…
I remember the days of old; I meditate on all thy works; I muse on the work of thy hands.
Will the Lord cast off for ever? and will he be favourable no more?
For the Lord will not cast off for ever:
For the Lord will not cast off his people, neither will he forsake his inheritance.
And the Lord will set his hand again the second time to restore his people from their lost and fallen state. Wherefore, he will proceed to do a marvelous work and a wonder among the children of men.
The Lord is King for ever and ever: the heathen are perished out of his land.
He will swallow up death in victory; and the Lord God will wipe away tears from off all faces; and the rebuke of his people shall he take away from off all the earth: for the Lord hath spoken it.
Is his mercy clean gone for ever? doth his promise fail for evermore?
My mercy will I keep for him for evermore, and my covenant shall stand fast with him.
Let Israel now say, that his mercy endureth for ever.
Let the house of Aaron now say, that his mercy endureth for ever.
And now I say unto you, that mercy hath no claim on that man; therefore his final doom is to endure a never-ending torment.

Yes, his mercy yearns over the Nation that has striven for a score
of years to rid the earth of the Priesthood of the Son of God and to
destroy the last Saint. He has mercy upon them, he bears with them, he
pleads with them by his Spirit, and occasionally sends his angels to
administer to them. Marvel not, then, that I pray for every soul that
can be saved. 8:124. {561}
Hath God forgotten to be gracious? hath he in anger shut up his tender mercies? Selah.
I remembered God, and was troubled: I complained, and my spirit was overwhelmed. Selah.
And the heavens shall declare his righteousness: for God is judge himself. Selah.
Thou therefore, O Lord God of hosts, the God of Israel, awake to visit all the heathen: be not merciful to any wicked transgressors. Selah.
And I said, This is my infirmity: but I will remember the years of the right hand of the most High.
59 And behold, my children, ye are a third generation. In my hundred and eighteenth year Joseph died.
I said, O my God, take me not away in the midst of my days: thy years are throughout all generations.

16. And ye are to be taught from on high. Sanctify yourselves and
    ye shall be endowed with power, that ye may give even as I have
    spoken.
I will remember the works of the Lord: surely I will remember thy wonders of old.
Shall thy wonders be known in the dark? and thy righteousness in the land of forgetfulness?
I remembered thy judgments of old, O Lord; and have comforted myself.

Behold, my sons, I desire that ye should remember to keep the commandments of God; and I would that ye should declare unto the people these words. Behold, I have given unto you the names of our first parents who came out of the land of Jerusalem; and this I have done that when you remember your names ye may remember them; and when ye remember them ye may remember their works; and when ye remember…
Wherefore I will not be negligent to put you always in remembrance of these things, though ye know them, and be established in the present truth.
I will meditate also of all thy work, and talk of thy doings.
I will meditate in thy precepts, and have respect unto thy ways.
Commit thy works unto the Lord, and thy thoughts shall be established.
Meditate upon these things; give thyself wholly to them; that thy profiting may appear to all.
Thy way, O God, is in the sanctuary: who is so great a God as our God?
That prepareth his heart to seek God, the Lord God of his fathers, though he be not cleansed according to the purification of the sanctuary.
Neither shall he go out of the sanctuary, nor profane the sanctuary of his God; for the crown of the anointing oil of his God is upon him: I am the Lord.
Praise ye the Lord. Praise God in his sanctuary: praise him in the firmament of his power.
Who is like unto thee, O Lord, among the gods? who is like thee, glorious in holiness, fearful in praises, doing wonders?
For the Lord is a great God, and a great King above all gods.
Thou art the God that doest wonders: thou hast declared thy strength among the people.
Thy God hath commanded thy strength: strengthen, O God, that which thou hast wrought for us.
For thou art great, and doest wondrous things: thou art God alone.
Be thou exalted, Lord, in thine own strength: so will we sing and praise thy power.
All they shall speak and say unto thee: Art thou also become weak as we? Art thou become like unto us?
I am as a wonder unto many; but thou art my strong refuge.
Thou hast with thine arm redeemed thy people, the sons of Jacob and Joseph. Selah.
But if they will not, make bare thine arm, O Lord, and redeem that which thou didst appoint a Zion unto thy people.
But now thus saith the Lord that created thee, O Jacob, and he that formed thee, O Israel, Fear not: for I have redeemed thee, I have called thee by thy name; thou art mine.
¶ But thou hast not called upon me, O Jacob; but thou hast been weary of me, O Israel.
Fear not, thou worm Jacob, and ye men of Israel; I will help thee, saith the Lord, and thy redeemer, the Holy One of Israel.
The waters saw thee, O God, the waters saw thee; they were afraid: the depths also were troubled.

"When through the deep waters I call thee to go,
    The rivers of sorrow shall not thee o'er flow."

And it came to pass that when they were buried in the deep there was no water that could hurt them, their vessels being tight like unto a dish, and also they were tight like unto the ark of Noah; therefore when they were encompassed about by many waters they did cry unto the Lord, and he did bring them forth again upon the top of the waters.
And the Gods also said: Let there be an expanse in the midst of the waters, and it shall divide the waters from the waters.
And thou didst divide the sea before them, so that they went through the midst of the sea on the dry land; and their persecutors thou threwest into the deeps, as a stone into the mighty waters.
The clouds poured out water: the skies sent out a sound: thine arrows also went abroad.
And it shall come to pass, when I bring a cloud over the earth, that the bow shall be seen in the cloud:
And he sent out arrows, and scattered them; lightning, and discomfited them.

And as I said unto you, as they were going about rebelling against God, behold, the angel of the Lord appeared unto them; and he descended as it were in a cloud; and he spake as it were with a voice of thunder, which caused the earth to shake upon which they stood;
The voice of thy thunder was in the heaven: the lightnings lightened the world: the earth trembled and shook.
The Lord thundered from heaven, and the most High uttered his voice.
And also cometh the testimony of the voice of thunderings, and the voice of lightnings, and the voice of tempests, and the voice of the waves of the sea heaving themselves beyond their bounds.
The Lord also thundered in the heavens, and the Highest gave his voice; hail stones and coals of fire.
Whose voice then shook the earth: but now he hath promised, saying, Yet once more I shake not the earth only, but also heaven.
His lightnings enlightened the world: the earth saw, and trembled.
Thy way is in the sea, and thy path in the great waters, and thy footsteps are not known.
Ponder the path of thy feet, and let all thy ways be established.

Yet thou in thy manifold mercies forsookest them not in the wilderness: the pillar of the cloud departed not from them by day, to lead them in the way; neither the pillar of fire by night, to shew them light, and the way wherein they should go.
Thus saith the Lord, which maketh a way in the sea, and a path in the mighty waters;
Thou didst walk through the sea with thine horses, through the heap of great waters.
And they shall wander from sea to sea, and from the north even to the east, they shall run to and fro to seek the word of the Lord, and shall not find it.
Thou leddest thy people like a flock by the hand of Moses and Aaron.
And Moses said unto Aaron, What did this people unto thee, that thou hast brought so great a sin upon them?
And Moses called unto them; and Aaron and all the rulers of the congregation returned unto him: and Moses talked with them.
And Aaron said unto Moses, Alas, my lord, I beseech thee, lay not the sin upon us, wherein we have done foolishly, and wherein we have sinned.
For which cause both thou and all thy company are gathered together against the Lord: and what is Aaron, that ye murmur against him?
And the whole congregation of the children of Israel murmured against Moses and Aaron in the wilderness:
